page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the n2-highcpu-32 in the average tier for most processing and memory tasks, with some notable exceptions. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance registers at 29778.72, while the single-core stress-ng score is 1409.48. Large block memory write bandwidth is a relative strength, scoring in the top 25% at 91.03 GB/sec, whereas large block read bandwidth remains average at 177.33 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 284.06 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model, though prompt processing for the same model is average at 1608.86 tokens per second.

The primary tradeoff of this server configuration is its highly skewed resource allocation, prioritizing raw compute capacity over memory. With only 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, it is not designed for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. Qualitatively, the instance offers high compute density for parallelizable workloads that do not require large memory footprints, making it cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, batch processing, web serving, and lightweight text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 1.0 GB per core memory allocation. Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 16 physical cores, this instance lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average range, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 9659.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 28275.53. However, it exhibits strong performance in large-block memory write bandwidth at 91.03 GB/sec and small-model text generation at 284.06 tokens per second. Due to its low memory-to-CPU ratio, the server is best suited for compute-bound, parallel workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and batch processing rather than memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. It is designed for CPU-heavy workloads like software compilation and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-32 features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, optimized for compute-heavy parallel workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and a lean memory profile for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
32.0 GB of system memory, providing a lean 1.0 GB per core ratio
Strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 91.03 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or graphics processing units
Suited for CPU-bound tasks, software compilation, and web serving
